name: 42 · Live Audio Stream (paced data channel + ?follow=1 player)
description:
A producer paces an uploaded WAV out over a Data/Out channel `speech` in ~real time (one ~200 ms PCM slice, then sleep ~200 ms), so the episode lasts about as long as the audio. Nothing in the net consumes the channel — the point is the out-of-band JetStream datastream, which the UI taps LIVE via `GET /api/v1/executions/{exec}/channels/speech/data?follow=1` and plays through Web Audio as it arrives, WHILE the step is still producing. The net only ever sees the channel's open + close (2 firings); the bulk audio never enters the marking.

Start{audio: file} → streamer (retrieve WAV, write paced PCM over `speech`) → End. Run with one command — `mekhan run 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000420 --start-tokens demos/42-live-audio-stream/sample-input.json` (5.5 s clip) or `--start-tokens demos/42-live-audio-stream/sample-long-input.json` (20.5 s, more dramatic). While the instance is running, open it in the app, find the `speech` channel, and hit Play live — the audio plays as it streams. This is the live-playback counterpart to demo 36 (which transcribes a data channel); here the same data plane is the source for a live media player.
